text: ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 An Israeli warning strike targeted the southern suburbs of Beirut. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇷 Iran’s Attorney General Movahedi-Azad said more than 7,000 public reports regarding espionage and individuals linked to foreign countries have been received by the judiciary’s reporting system. Necessary orders have been issued to review and process the reports quickly. He praised the public’s solidarity and cooperation following recent events, noting that the "martyrdom" of the nation’s leader has strengthened national unity. @wfwitness
⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 The IDF has warned it will soon strike Lebanon’s Al Masnaa border crossing with Syria in the east of the country, over its alleged use by Hezbollah to smuggle military equipment. @wfwitness

The arrested elements provided Israel with: 🔸 Military and security coordinates 🔸 Locations of military and security sites 🔸 Intelligence on economic facilities inside Yemen They operated using espionage applications and covert communication programs. Issued April 4, 2026. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

🇮🇷 🔥 Ilya Anvari-Maram, a fourth-grade student from Kermanshah, addresses the enemy: "O Israel — O wicked and vile enemy! Know that the soil of Iran is nourished by blood. Every stone is forceful — we stand steadfast to the death, without submission, without shame. We have joined hands to dry up the roots of oppression, to raise the banner of truth!" 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 🇸🇾 Kan : Hezbollah is suffering from tensions and a lack of coordination between senior command and field operatives. While the group feels it has surprised those who wrote it off, behind the scenes it faces problems including limited rocket stockpiles, difficulties replenishing supplies, and field units sometimes inflating achievements or firing more rockets than ordered. In Syria, the al-Sharaa government is playing a double game regarding pro‑Palestinian protests that erupted following the Knesset’s approval of a death penalty law for militants. Authorities are allowing demonstrations to avoid appearing as Israeli collaborators but are preventing protesters from approaching the Israeli border to avoid friction. A Syrian official warned that if Israel continues to disregard Syria’s interests, it could open the door to provocations. @wfwitness

🔄 Forwarded from /CIG/ Telegram | Counter Intelligence Global: 🇮🇷 ⚔️ 🇮🇱 Earlier today, an Iranian missile hit the parking lot in the vicinity of the Israeli ministry of defense in HaKyria, Tel Aviv. 🔗 EGYOSINT

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 Kan : An Israeli source has said at least another week of intense fighting with Iran lies ahead, as ceasefire talks between Washington and Tehran have reached a dead end. The U.S. is reportedly planning to strike Iranian national infrastructure. U.S. President Trump issued a 48‑hour ultimatum, warning that “the gates of hell will open” if no deal is reached. The Israeli Air Force struck petrochemical plants in Khuzestan, south‑western Iran, a strike confirmed by Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu and the IDF. @wfwitness

Visioner RT @NSTRIKE1231 : ‼️ 🇺🇲 🇮🇷 BREAKING | At this moment, reports are emerging that U.S. evacuation teams have entered southwestern Iran, specifically in the Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad province, where they are conducting a highly risky and dangerous search-and-rescue operation to recover an American pilot. Iranian sources report that intense exchanges of fire have already been recorded. U.S. evacuation teams have reportedly engaged Basij and IRGC fighters from helicopters, targeting forces attempting to interfere with the operation and disrupt safe air movement. See the latest updates with us: @NSTRIKE1231 tweet

⚡️ 🇾🇪 🇮🇱 Yemen’s Houthi security services have arrested a number of individuals who allegedly worked directly with Israeli intelligence, including the military intelligence branch (Aman), the Mossad and other Israeli entities. The detainees allegedly carried out espionage activities by providing military and security information, coordinates of military and security sites, and details on economic infrastructure. The suspects used espionage programmes and encrypted communication tools. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 The Israeli Air Force, acting on military intelligence, struck a petrochemical complex in Mahshahr, south‑western Iran, which was responsible for producing chemicals for the "regime’s weapons." The site was one of two central facilities for manufacturing materials used in explosives, ballistic missiles and other arms. Damage to this infrastructure will disrupt Iran’s ability to produce weapons designed to target Israel and other Middle Eastern countries. The strike is part of the campaign to deepen the impact on Iran’s military foundations. @wfwitness
⚡️ 🇱🇧 🇺🇳 🇮🇱 UNIFIL spokesperson: Israeli soldiers destroyed all the cameras facing our headquarters in Naqoura. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇸🇾 🇮🇱 Local sources in Daraa reported hearing multiple explosions in the western part of Daraa Governorate coinciding with Israeli jets flying above. Some sources also reported a vehicle carrying war armament remnants exploded which caused the confusion which might have been the cause of the noise the other sources reported. It's reported three individuals were killed from the explosion. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu said Israel continues to pound the Iranian government. After destroying 70% of Iran’s steel production capacity, which provides raw materials for weapons, Israel today struck Iranian petrochemical plants. “These two things are the regime’s money machine to fuel its war of terror against us and against the entire world,” he said. “We will continue to pound them.” @wfwitness
⚡ 🇮🇱 🇾🇪 Initial reports of a launch detected from Yemen targeting Israel. This event appears to have been resolved. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 Israeli Channel 13: Israel is preparing for strikes on Iranian energy facilities, awaiting a green light from the United States. The strikes could come as early as next week, following the expiry of U.S. President Trump’s ultimatum to Iran. A senior cabinet discussion was held yesterday to approve additional targets, and the full security cabinet is expected to meet tomorrow. The last cabinet meeting included a sharp warning from the IDF chief of staff. Israel assesses that the green light will be given if the ultimatum expires and negotiations collapse. @wfwitness

🇮🇳 🇮🇷 India has bought Iranian oil for the first time since 2019. The purchase of 600,000 barrels loaded was at Kharg Island, and paid for in Indian Rupees. It is arriving at Gujarat's Vadinar refinery today. The purchase was made possible by a narrow 30-day US sanctions waiver expiring April 19. India also took delivery of 44,000 tons of Iranian LPG at Mangalore this week, loaded on a sanctioned vessel. At its peak, Iran supplied 16% of India's crude. The US "maximum pressure" campaign killed that in 2019. Now the same US is quietly reopening the door because the Strait of Hormuz closure is hitting the global economy too hard to ignore. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics
NEW: India has bought Iranian oil for the first time since 2019. The U.S. temporarily eased sanctions, allowing the purchase.
Netanyahu on Iran: After we destroyed 70% of their steel production capacity, today we attacked their petrochemical plants. We will continue to crush them.
🇮🇷 The IRGC is dedicating drones to TUPAC!! Amazing things are happening. What a time to be alive! 🤣 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

⚡️ 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 🇮🇱 Hezbollah’s deputy political chief Mahmoud Qamati claimed the group has successfully repelled Israeli aggression despite the deployment of over 100,000 Israeli soldiers backed by tanks and aircraft. He said Israeli forces have not been able to hold any point inside Lebanese territory, and that around 150 Israeli tanks have been destroyed or disabled. Qamati stated that Hezbollah’s flexible tactics, based on urban warfare and hit‑and‑run operations, are raising attrition costs for the IDF. He added that the group has prepared for a long war and will accept no agreement without a full Israeli withdrawal from Lebanese land. @wfwitness
📸 Iranian drone dedicated to Tupac.

🇮🇷 "CENTCOM has literally gone silent. One starts to wonder — could it be that their account admin was stationed at one of the regional bases and got hit?" Iranian military analysts discuss yesterday's operation and the silence that followed. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics
